     8→use tower_mcp::protocol::{
     9→    CallToolParams, CompleteParams, CompleteResult, Completion, CompletionReference, McpRequest,
    10→};
    11→use tower_mcp::router::{RouterRequest, RouterResponse};
    12→use tower_mcp::{HttpTransport, McpRouter, McpTracingLayer, StdioTransport};
    13→use tower_resilience::bulkhead::BulkheadLayer;
    14→use tower_resilience::cache::SharedCacheLayer;
    15→use tower_resilience::ratelimiter::RateLimiterLayer;
    16→
    17→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, ValueEnum)]
    18→enum Transport {
    19→    Stdio,
    20→    Http,
    21→}
    22→
    23→#[derive(Parser, Debug)]
    24→#[command(name = "cratesio-mcp")]
    25→#[command(about = "MCP server for querying crates.io", long_about = None)]
    26→struct Args {
    27→    /// Transport to use
    28→    #[arg(short, long, default_value = "stdio")]
    29→    transport: Transport,
    30→
    31→    /// Maximum concurrent requests (concurrency limit)
    32→    #[arg(long, default_value = "10")]
    33→    max_concurrent: usize,
    34→
    35→    /// Rate limit interval between crates.io API calls (in milliseconds)
    36→    #[arg(long, default_value = "1000")]
    37→    rate_limit_ms: u64,
    38→
    39→    /// Log level
    40→    #[arg(short, long, default_value = "info")]
    41→    log_level: String,
    42→
    43→    /// HTTP host to bind to (use 0.0.0.0 for public access)
    44→    #[arg(long, default_value = "127.0.0.1")]
    45→    host: String,
    46→
    47→    /// HTTP port to bind to
    48→    #[arg(short, long, default_value = "3000")]
    49→    port: u16,
    50→
    51→    /// Request timeout in seconds (for HTTP transport)
    52→    #[arg(long, default_value = "30")]
    53→    request_timeout_secs: u64,
    54→
    55→    /// Minimal mode - only register tools (no prompts, resources, or completions).
    56→    /// Use this to work around Claude Code MCP tool discovery issues.
    57→    /// See: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/2682
    58→    #[arg(long, default_value = "false")]
    59→    minimal: bool,
    60→
    61→    /// Enable response caching for tool calls (HTTP transport only)
    62→    #[arg(long, default_value = "true")]
    63→    cache_enabled: bool,
    64→
    65→    /// Cache TTL in seconds (how long cached responses are valid)
    66→    #[arg(long, default_value = "300")]
    67→    cache_ttl_secs: u64,
    68→
    69→    /// Maximum number of cached responses
    70→    #[arg(long, default_value = "200")]
    71→    cache_max_size: usize,
    72→
    73→    /// Maximum number of cached docs.rs rustdoc JSON entries
    74→    #[arg(long, default_value = "10")]
    75→    docs_cache_max_entries: usize,
    76→
    77→    /// TTL for cached docs.rs rustdoc JSON entries (in seconds)
    78→    #[arg(long, default_value = "3600")]
    79→    docs_cache_ttl_secs: u64,
    80→}
    81→
    82→#[tokio::main]
    83→async fn main() -> Result<(), tower_mcp::BoxError> {
    84→    let args = Args::parse();
    85→
    86→    // Initialize tracing
    87→    tracing_subscriber::fmt()
    88→        .with_env_filter(
    89→            tracing_subscriber::EnvFilter::from_default_env()
    90→                .add_directive(format!("cratesio_mcp={}", args.log_level).parse()?)
    91→                .add_directive(format!("tower_mcp={}", args.log_level).parse()?),
    92→        )
    93→        .with_writer(std::io::stderr)
    94→        .init();
    95→
    96→    tracing::info!(
    97→        transport = ?args.transport,
    98→        max_concurrent = args.max_concurrent,
    99→        rate_limit_ms = args.rate_limit_ms,
   100→        "Starting cratesio-mcp server"
   101→    );
   102→
   103→    // Create shared state with rate limiting for crates.io API
   104→    let rate_limit = Duration::from_millis(args.rate_limit_ms);
   105→    let docs_cache_ttl = Duration::from_secs(args.docs_cache_ttl_secs);
   106→    let state = Arc::new(
   107→        AppState::new(rate_limit, args.docs_cache_max_entries, docs_cache_ttl)
   108→            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to create state: {}", e))?,
   109→    );
   110→
   111→    // Build all tools
   112→    let search_tool = tools::search::build(state.clone());
   113→    let info_tool = tools::info::build(state.clone());
   114→    let versions_tool = tools::versions::build(state.clone());
   115→    let deps_tool = tools::dependencies::build(state.clone());
   116→    let reverse_deps_tool = tools::reverse_deps::build(state.clone());
   117→    let downloads_tool = tools::downloads::build(state.clone());
   118→    let owners_tool = tools::owners::build(state.clone());
   119→    let summary_tool = tools::summary::build(state.clone());
   120→    let authors_tool = tools::authors::build(state.clone());
   121→    let user_tool = tools::user::build(state.clone());
   122→    let readme_tool = tools::readme::build(state.clone());
   123→    let categories_tool = tools::categories::build(state.clone());
   124→    let keywords_tool = tools::keywords::build(state.clone());
   125→    let version_downloads_tool = tools::version_downloads::build(state.clone());
   126→    let version_detail_tool = tools::version_detail::build(state.clone());
   127→    let category_tool = tools::category::build(state.clone());
   128→    let keyword_detail_tool = tools::keyword_detail::build(state.clone());
   129→    let get_crate_docs_tool = tools::crate_docs::build(state.clone());
   130→    let get_doc_item_tool = tools::doc_item::build(state.clone());
   131→    let search_docs_tool = tools::search_docs::build(state.clone());
   132→    let audit_tool = tools::audit::build(state.clone());
   133→    let features_tool = tools::features::build(state.clone());
   134→    let user_stats_tool = tools::user_stats::build(state.clone());
   135→    let compare_tool = tools::compare::build(state.clone());
   136→    let dependency_tree_tool = tools::dependency_tree::build(state.clone());
   137→    let health_check_tool = tools::health_check::build(state.clone());
   138→
   139→    // Create base router with tools (always registered)
   140→    let instructions = if args.minimal {
   141→        "MCP server for querying crates.io - the Rust package registry.\n\n\
   142→         Available tools:\n\
   143→         - search_crates: Find crates by name/keywords\n\
   144→         - get_crate_info: Get detailed crate information\n\
   145→         - get_crate_versions: Get version history\n\
   146→         - get_crate_readme: Get README content for a crate\n\
   147→         - get_dependencies: Get dependencies for a version\n\
   148→         - get_reverse_dependencies: Find crates that depend on this crate\n\
   149→         - get_downloads: Get download statistics\n\
   150→         - get_owners: Get crate owners/maintainers\n\
   151→         - get_summary: Get crates.io global statistics\n\
   152→         - get_crate_authors: Get authors for a crate version\n\
   153→         - get_user: Get a user's profile\n\
   154→         - get_categories: Browse crates.io categories\n\
   155→         - get_keywords: Browse crates.io keywords\n\
   156→         - get_version_downloads: Daily download stats for a specific version\n\
   157→         - get_crate_version: Detailed metadata for a specific version\n\
   158→         - get_category: Details about a specific category\n\
   159→         - get_keyword: Details about a specific keyword\n\
   160→         - get_crate_docs: Browse crate documentation structure from docs.rs\n\
   161→         - get_doc_item: Get full documentation for a specific item from docs.rs\n\
   162→         - search_docs: Search for items by name within a crate's docs\n\
   163→         - audit_dependencies: Check deps against OSV.dev vulnerability database\n\
   164→         - get_crate_features: Get feature flags for a crate version\n\
   165→         - get_user_stats: Get download statistics for a crates.io user\n\
   166→         - compare_crates: Compare two or more crates side by side\n\
   167→         - get_dependency_tree: Get full transitive dependency tree for a crate\n\
   168→         - crate_health_check: Comprehensive health report for a crate\n\n\
   169→         (Running in minimal mode - resources, prompts, and completions disabled)"
   170→    } else {
   171→        "MCP server for querying crates.io - the Rust package registry.\n\n\
   172→         Available tools:\n\
   173→         - search_crates: Find crates by name/keywords\n\
   174→         - get_crate_info: Get detailed crate information\n\
   175→         - get_crate_versions: Get version history\n\
   176→         - get_crate_readme: Get README content for a crate\n\
   177→         - get_dependencies: Get dependencies for a version\n\
   178→         - get_reverse_dependencies: Find crates that depend on this crate\n\
   179→         - get_downloads: Get download statistics\n\
   180→         - get_owners: Get crate owners/maintainers\n\
   181→         - get_summary: Get crates.io global statistics\n\
   182→         - get_crate_authors: Get authors for a crate version\n\
   183→         - get_user: Get a user's profile\n\
   184→         - get_categories: Browse crates.io categories\n\
   185→         - get_keywords: Browse crates.io keywords\n\
   186→         - get_version_downloads: Daily download stats for a specific version\n\
   187→         - get_crate_version: Detailed metadata for a specific version\n\
   188→         - get_category: Details about a specific category\n\
   189→         - get_keyword: Details about a specific keyword\n\
   190→         - get_crate_docs: Browse crate documentation structure from docs.rs\n\
   191→         - get_doc_item: Get full documentation for a specific item from docs.rs\n\
   192→         - search_docs: Search for items by name within a crate's docs\n\
   193→         - audit_dependencies: Check deps against OSV.dev vulnerability database\n\
   194→         - get_crate_features: Get feature flags for a crate version\n\
   195→         - get_user_stats: Get download statistics for a crates.io user\n\
   196→         - compare_crates: Compare two or more crates side by side\n\
   197→         - get_dependency_tree: Get full transitive dependency tree for a crate\n\
   198→         - crate_health_check: Comprehensive health report for a crate\n\n\
   199→         Resources:\n\
   200→         - crates://{name}/info: Get crate info as a resource\n\
   201→         - crates://{name}/readme: Get README content for a crate\n\
   202→         - crates://{name}/docs: Get documentation structure for a crate\n\n\
   203→         Use the prompts for guided analysis:\n\
   204→         - analyze_crate: Comprehensive crate analysis\n\
   205→         - compare_crates: Compare multiple crates\n\
   206→         - stack_review: Evaluate a set of crates as a cohesive stack\n\
   207→         - evaluate_dependencies: Evaluate project dependencies for health and security\n\
   208→         - recommend_crates: Find and evaluate crates for a use case\n\
   209→         - migration_guide: Generate a migration guide between two crates"
   210→    };
   211→
   212→    let mut router = McpRouter::new()
   213→        .server_info("cratesio-mcp", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"))
   214→        .instructions(instructions)
   215→        .tool(search_tool)
   216→        .tool(info_tool)
   217→        .tool(versions_tool)
   218→        .tool(deps_tool)
   219→        .tool(reverse_deps_tool)
   220→        .tool(downloads_tool)
   221→        .tool(owners_tool)
   222→        .tool(summary_tool)
   223→        .tool(authors_tool)
   224→        .tool(user_tool)
   225→        .tool(readme_tool)
   226→        .tool(categories_tool)
   227→        .tool(keywords_tool)
   228→        .tool(version_downloads_tool)
   229→        .tool(version_detail_tool)
   230→        .tool(category_tool)
   231→        .tool(keyword_detail_tool)
   232→        .tool(get_crate_docs_tool)
   233→        .tool(get_doc_item_tool)
   234→        .tool(search_docs_tool)
   235→        .tool(audit_tool)
   236→        .tool(features_tool)
   237→        .tool(user_stats_tool)
   238→        .tool(compare_tool)
   239→        .tool(dependency_tree_tool)
   240→        .tool(health_check_tool);
   241→
   242→    // Add resources, prompts, and completions unless in minimal mode
   243→    // Minimal mode works around Claude Code MCP tool discovery issues
   244→    // See: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/2682
   245→    if !args.minimal {
   246→        // Build resources
   247→        let recent_searches = resources::recent_searches::build(state.clone());
   248→        let crate_info_template = resources::crate_info::build(state.clone());
   249→        let readme_template = resources::readme::build(state.clone());
   250→        let docs_template = resources::docs::build(state.clone());
   251→
   252→        // Build prompts
   253→        let analyze_prompt = prompts::analyze::build();
   254→        let compare_prompt = prompts::compare::build();
   255→        let stack_review_prompt = prompts::stack_review::build();
   256→        let evaluate_dependencies_prompt = prompts::evaluate_dependencies::build();
   257→        let recommend_prompt = prompts::recommend::build();
   258→        let migration_guide_prompt = prompts::migration_guide::build();
   259→
   260→        // Popular crates for completion suggestions
   261→        let popular_crates = vec![
   262→            "serde",
   263→            "tokio",
   264→            "anyhow",
   265→            "thiserror",
   266→            "clap",
   267→            "tracing",
   268→            "reqwest",
   269→            "axum",
   270→            "tower",
   271→            "hyper",
   272→            "futures",
   273→            "async-trait",
   274→            "rand",
   275→            "regex",
   276→            "chrono",
   277→            "uuid",
   278→            "log",
   279→            "env_logger",
   280→            "syn",
   281→            "quote",
   282→            "proc-macro2",
   283→            "bytes",
   284→            "http",
   285→            "tonic",
   286→            "prost",
   287→            "sqlx",
   288→            "diesel",
   289→            "actix-web",
   290→            "rocket",
   291→            "warp",
   292→            "tide",
   293→            "poem",
   294→            "salvo",
   295→        ];
   296→
   297→        router = router
   298→            .resource(recent_searches)
   299→            .resource_template(crate_info_template)
   300→            .resource_template(readme_template)
   301→            .resource_template(docs_template)
   302→            .prompt(analyze_prompt)
   303→            .prompt(compare_prompt)
   304→            .prompt(stack_review_prompt)
   305→            .prompt(evaluate_dependencies_prompt)
   306→            .prompt(recommend_prompt)
   307→            .prompt(migration_guide_prompt)
   308→            // Completion handler for crate name suggestions
   309→            .completion_handler(move |params: CompleteParams| {
   310→                let popular = popular_crates.clone();
   311→                async move {
   312→                    let prefix = params.argument.value.to_lowercase();
   313→
   314→                    // Filter popular crates by prefix
   315→                    let suggestions: Vec<String> = popular
   316→                        .iter()
   317→                        .filter(|name| name.starts_with(&prefix))
   318→                        .take(10)
   319→                        .map(|name| name.to_string())
   320→                        .collect();
   321→
   322→                    // Log what we're completing for
   323→                    match &params.reference {
   324→                        CompletionReference::Prompt { name } => {
   325→                            tracing::debug!(%name, %prefix, "Completing prompt argument");
   326→                        }
   327→                        CompletionReference::Resource { uri } => {
   328→                            tracing::debug!(%uri, %prefix, "Completing resource URI");
   329→                        }
   330→                        _ => {
   331→                            tracing::debug!(%prefix, "Completing unknown reference type");
   332→                        }
   333→                    }
   334→
   335→                    Ok(CompleteResult {
   336→                        completion: Completion {
   337→                            values: suggestions,
   338→                            total: None,
   339→                            has_more: Some(false),
   340→                        },
   341→                        meta: None,
   342→                    })
   343→                }
   344→            });
   345→
   346→        tracing::info!("Full mode: resources, prompts, and completions enabled");
   347→    } else {
   348→        tracing::info!(
   349→            "Minimal mode: only tools registered (workaround for Claude Code MCP issues)"
   350→        );
   351→    }
   352→
   353→    let router = router;
   354→
   355→    match args.transport {
   356→        Transport::Stdio => {
   357→            // For stdio, we serve directly without middleware since error handling
   358→            // is more complex (would need error type conversion).
   359→            tracing::info!("Serving over stdio");
   360→            StdioTransport::new(router).run().await?;
   361→        }
   362→        Transport::Http => {
   363→            let addr = format!("{}:{}", args.host, args.port);
   364→            tracing::info!(
   365→                %addr,
   366→                cache_enabled = args.cache_enabled,
   367→                cache_ttl_secs = args.cache_ttl_secs,
   368→                cache_max_size = args.cache_max_size,
   369→                "Serving over HTTP"
   370→            );
   371→
   372→            // Build tower middleware stack for request protection:
   373→            //
   374→            // 1. TimeoutLayer - Request timeout protection
   375→            // 2. RateLimiterLayer - Limits requests per second (token bucket)
   376→            // 3. BulkheadLayer - Limits concurrent in-flight requests
   377→            // 4. CacheLayer - Response caching for tool calls (optional)
   378→            //
   379→            // These layers compose naturally with tower-mcp's Service implementation.
   380→            // The HTTP transport's CatchError wrapper converts middleware errors
   381→            // to JSON-RPC error responses.
   382→            //
   383→            // tower-resilience layers use composite error types that wrap both
   384→            // the layer's own errors and the inner service error, making them
   385→            // compatible with tower-mcp's Infallible error type.
   386→            //
   387→            // Note: CircuitBreakerLayer could be added for downstream service failures
   388→            // (e.g., crates.io API), but McpRouter returns Infallible so the breaker
   389→            // would need a custom failure classifier to inspect response content.
   390→            let rate_limiter = RateLimiterLayer::builder()
   391→                .limit_for_period(10) // 10 requests per second
   392→                .refresh_period(Duration::from_secs(1))
   393→                .timeout_duration(Duration::from_millis(500))
   394→                .build();
   395→
   396→            let bulkhead = BulkheadLayer::builder()
   397→                .max_concurrent_calls(args.max_concurrent)
   398→                .max_wait_duration(Duration::from_millis(500))
   399→                .build();
   400→
   401→            // Response caching for tool calls using SharedCacheLayer.
   402→            // SharedCacheLayer shares the cache store across all layer() calls,
   403→            // so all HTTP sessions share the same cache (unlike regular CacheLayer).
   404→            // The key extractor creates cache keys only for tool calls (tools/call).
   405→            // Other MCP methods (list_tools, initialize, ping) get unique keys
   406→            // that never match, effectively bypassing the cache.
   407→            let cache: SharedCacheLayer<RouterRequest, String, RouterResponse> =
   408→                SharedCacheLayer::builder()
   409→                    .max_size(args.cache_max_size)
   410→                    .ttl(Duration::from_secs(args.cache_ttl_secs))
   411→                    .key_extractor(|req: &RouterRequest| -> String {
   412→                        // Only cache tool calls - create deterministic key from tool name + args
   413→                        match &req.inner {
   414→                            McpRequest::CallTool(CallToolParams {
   415→                                name, arguments, ..
   416→                            }) => {
   417→                                // Serialize arguments to create stable cache key
   418→                                let args_str = serde_json::to_string(arguments).unwrap_or_default();
   419→                                format!("tool:{}:{}", name, args_str)
   420→                            }
   421→                            // For all other requests, use unique key based on request ID
   422→                            // This ensures they're never cached (each request ID is unique)
   423→                            _ => format!("nocache:{:?}", req.id),
   424→                        }
   425→                    })
   426→                    .on_hit(|| tracing::debug!("Cache hit"))
   427→                    .on_miss(|| tracing::debug!("Cache miss"))
   428→                    .build();
   429→
   430→            let builder = ServiceBuilder::new()
   431→                // Outer layers (applied first on request, last on response)
   432→                .layer(TimeoutLayer::new(Duration::from_secs(
   433→                    args.request_timeout_secs,
   434→                )))
   435→                .layer(rate_limiter)
   436→                .layer(bulkhead);
   437→
   438→            // Conditionally add cache layer
   439→            // optional_sessions() allows clients that don't carry mcp-session-id
   440→            // forward (e.g. Codex CLI, Cursor) to still call tools.
   441→            // See: https://github.com/joshrotenberg/cratesio-mcp/issues/61
   442→            let transport = if args.cache_enabled {
   443→                HttpTransport::new(router)
   444→                    .disable_origin_validation()
   445→                    .optional_sessions()
   446→                    .layer(
   447→                        builder
   448→                            .layer(cache)
   449→                            .layer(McpTracingLayer::new())
   450→                            .into_inner(),
   451→                    )
   452→            } else {
   453→                HttpTransport::new(router)
   454→                    .disable_origin_validation()
   455→                    .optional_sessions()
   456→                    .layer(builder.layer(McpTracingLayer::new()).into_inner())
   457→            };
   458→
   459→            transport.serve(&addr).await?;
   460→        }
   461→    }
   462→
   463→    Ok(())
   464→}
   465→

use schemars::JsonSchema;

use tower_mcp::{CallToolResult, Tool, ToolBuilder, extract::{Json, State}};
use crate::state::AppState;

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
pub struct CompareInput {
    /// Comma-separated list of crate names to compare (2-5 crates)
    crates: String,
}

// 2. Build function
pub fn build(state: Arc<AppState>) -> Tool {
    ToolBuilder::new("compare_crates")
        .title("Compare Crates")
        .description("Compare two or more crates side by side...")
        .read_only()               // Mark as read-only
        .idempotent()              // Mark as idempotent
        .icon("https://crates.io/assets/cargo.png")
        
        // 3. Handler with extractors
        .extractor_handler(
            state,
            |State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>, 
             Json(input): Json<CompareInput>| async move {
                
                // 4. Parse input
                let names: Vec<&str> = input.crates.split(',').map(|s| s.trim()).collect();
                
                // 5. Validate
                if names.len() < 2 {
                    return Ok(CallToolResult::text("Please provide at least 2 crate names"));
                }
                
                // 6. Call client methods
                for name in &names {
                    let info = state.client.get_crate(name).await;
                    // ... gather data
                }
                
                // 7. Format and return result
                Ok(CallToolResult::text(output))
            },
        )
        .build()
}
```

        version: &str
    ) -> Result<Version, Error> {
        let resp: VersionResponse = self
            .get_json(&format!("/crates/{name}/{version}"))
            .await?;
        Ok(resp.version)
    }
}

            .await
    }
    
    /// Get metadata for a specific version
    pub async fn crate_version(
        &self, 
        name: &str, 
        version: &str
    ) -> Result<Version, Error> {
        let resp: VersionResponse = self
            .get_json(&format!("/crates/{name}/{version}"))
            .await?;
        Ok(resp.version)
    }
}
```

**Patterns**:
- Use `impl CratesIoClient` to add methods to the client
- Methods use `self.get_json_query()`, `self.get_json()`, etc.
- Paths constructed with string formatting
- Query params built as `Vec<(String, String)>`
- Return `Result<T, Error>`
- Often wrap wire types to expose cleaner public types

### 9. DEPENDENCIES (Cargo.toml)

**Key dependencies**:
- `tower-mcp = "0.9.1"` - MCP framework with http + testing features
- `reqwest = "0.12"` - HTTP client with JSON + gzip
- `tokio = "1"` - Async runtime (full features)
- `tower-resilience = "0.7.1"` - Middleware (bulkhead, ratelimiter, cache)
- `schemars = "1"` - JSON schema generation from Rust types
- `serde/serde_json = "1"` - Serialization
- `chrono = "0.4"` - Date/time handling
- `thiserror = "2"` - Error types
- `wiremock = "0.6"` - HTTP mocking (dev-only)

**Edition**: 2024, MSRV 1.90

---

## Summary

The codebase uses a clean modular architecture:

1. **Client layer** (`src/client/`) - Low-level API calls with rate limiting, organized into impl blocks by endpoint category
2. **State layer** (`src/state.rs`) - Shared state with three clients (crates.io, docs.rs, OSV) and caches
3. **Tool layer** (`src/tools/`) - 29 independent MCP tool implementations following a consistent builder pattern
4. **Server layer** (`src/main.rs`) - Tool registration, middleware stack, transport setup (stdio/HTTP)

The tool pattern is highly consistent: define input struct → implement `build()` → use `ToolBuilder` with `extractor_handler` → call client methods → format and return `CallToolResult::text()`.

Testing uses wiremock with rate limiting disabled (0ms) for fast execution. Tests define mock responses as const JSON strings and verify both success and error cases.

//! Public data types for the crates.io API.

use std::collections::HashMap;

use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

// ── Core types ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/// Crate metadata from the crates.io API.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Crate {
    pub name: String,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub max_version: String,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub max_stable_version: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub downloads: u64,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub recent_downloads: Option<u64>,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub repository: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub documentation: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub homepage: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub keywords: Option<Vec<String>>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub categories: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

/// Version metadata from the crates.io API.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Version {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub id: u64,
    pub num: String,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub yanked: bool,
    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub downloads: u64,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub license: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub rust_version: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub features: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>,
}

/// Per-version download data point.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct VersionDownloads {
    pub version: u64,
    pub downloads: u64,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub date: Option<String>,
}

/// User or team on crates.io.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct User {
    pub id: u64,
    pub login: String,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub name: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub url: String,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub avatar: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub kind: Option<String>,
}

/// Dependency of a crate version.

PR created: https://github.com/joshrotenberg/cratesio-mcp/pull/72

Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**`get_crate_changelog` tool** — fetches changelog content from a crate's GitHub repository.

**Files changed:**
- `src/client/changelog.rs` (new): `fetch_changelog()` method on `CratesIoClient` + `parse_github_repo()` helper. Tries 8 common filenames via `raw.githubusercontent.com`.
- `src/client/mod.rs`: Added `github_raw_base_url` field and `with_github_raw_url()` builder for testability. The field defaults to `"https://raw.githubusercontent.com"`.
- `src/state.rs`: Added `AppState::with_changelog_urls()` test constructor for two-server wiremock tests.
- `src/tools/changelog.rs` (new): Tool handler + `extract_version_section()` for optional version filtering. Tests cover: changelog found, no repo URL, non-GitHub repo, no changelog found after all filenames, and version section extraction.
- `src/tools/mod.rs` + `src/main.rs`: Tool registered.
- `tests/mcp_integration.rs`: Tool added to test router, count updated to 23.

**119 lib tests + 39 integration tests all pass.**
